mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 2% within the 5-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and resilient household balance sheets, which supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform when either RSAFS or HOUST individually weakens.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost pressures and improves corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, resulting in a persistent negative correlation over a 12-month rolling window, or SPX declines despite consecutive months of INDPRO expansion above 105.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals an economic slowdown that compresses earnings expectations, triggering algorithmic risk reduction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for > 60% of quarters following GDP growth below 1.5%, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from baseline periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high federal funds rates and rising unemployment create a stagflationary feedback loop that erodes real corporate cash flows while elevating discount rates, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint high-rate/high-unemployment signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of observations, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from periods with only one of the two conditions present.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevation in the consumer price index erodes real household purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite CPI readings consistently above 3.5% year-over-year, or the signal exhibits a positive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity performance, resulting in a negative or zero correlation over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when PAYEMS exceeds 200k monthly additions.
